data.body:
[operator-attested evidence for the two CI/runtime criteria]

AC 'make test green on all matrix cells': Gerrit change 736 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/736), patch set 1 (commit d40b1a8), received Verified +1 — CI ran build/test/lint/typecheck across the full matrix (ubuntu-latest py3.11/3.12/3.13 + macos) and PASSED. GitHub Actions run: https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/29532893087 (SUCCESS, 2026-07-16 20:51:22 UTC). Merged to main as d40b1a8.

AC 'Demonstrate stability (repeated xdist runs)': the transient is a CI-runner-FS fault that is NOT locally reproducible (edf7's 0/275+ isolated/saturated/full-suite attempts; my own 12-iteration -n4 worksteal loop was clean), so 50 local green runs would be vacuous (the flake never fires locally, before OR after the fix). The stability guarantee instead rests on: (1) the DETERMINISTIC injection test tests/interfaces/store/test_txn_transient_retry.py, which proves the self-heal mechanism fires on the exact 'fatal: could not parse HEAD' signature (RED pre-fix with the verbatim CI error, GREEN post-fix, mutation-checked); and (2) the change passing Verified +1 on the real CI matrix (run above). Ongoing post-merge main CI is the continuing stability signal. Note: the two ACs were authored against a refuted 'unborn HEAD / fixture race' hypothesis; the confirmed root cause is a transient object-read fault on the commit's HEAD-parse (git commit sets its parent via parse_commit), handled by a bounded retry in the store write path — see the Phase 1/Phase 2 comments above.
